what_it_does: This file is a threaded conversation note between the ops/CLI session and the voice-stack session. It documents changes to the company CLI, the services registry, systemd units, and the negotiated decision to converge on a single shared VRAM resource core (cli/gpu.py) used by both model and voice services.
observations: ops/CLI made services.json pretty-printed and added a VRAM resource manager in cli/gpu.py
vLLM models were moved to a config-driven serve_model.sh launcher
voice services live in voice/ops/systemd/ and were deliberately not touched by ops initially
both sides identified two parallel VRAM managers (cli/gpu.py and voice/lifecycle.py) computing budgets independently
EngineCore orphan leak was traced to vLLM's lack of OS death-link; systemd cgroup stop reaps it cleanly
ears (stt-parakeet/canary/granite) were flipped from manual to user-unit in services.json
voice/lifecycle.py was refactored to import cli/gpu.py and launch/stop services via systemd units
cli/gpu.py exposes read_gpu, budget_of, check_fit, plan_eviction, and teardown
budget uses gpu_util*ceiling for config models and load.vram_mb for load services, with learned_vram telemetry overriding both
a ~/.company/.secrets file was created for provider keys in EnvironmentFile format
litellm-proxy.service is flagged as broken (missing WorkingDirectory and port mismatch)
a large interactive-surface branch was merged to main
standouts: vLLM EngineCore orphans VRAM unless reaped by a systemd cgroup stop, which is the deciding factor for unit-managed voice
the two independent VRAM managers converge on cli/gpu.py as the single shared core
lifecycle.py now delegates load/unload to systemd units instead of using Popen
live verification shows tts-orpheus and stt-parakeet start and stop cleanly with no orphan
conventions: uses threaded reply headers (REPLY, REPLY 2, REPLY 3, NOTE)
organizes discussion as numbered questions Q1-Q5
records boundaries of what was and was not touched
uses plain factual verification with baseline/free VRAM numbers
keeps module stdlib-only for bridge import safety
uses EnvironmentFile=-%h/company/.secrets for systemd secret injection
concerns: litellm-proxy.service points to a non-existent WorkingDirectory and port 4000 while the registry expects 4100
services.json concurrent-edit risk is flagged but unresolved
the file is a conversation log, so some statements may become stale as code evolves
no code symbols are present, only prose decisions and verification notes
notes: A cross-session decision log showing how the company CLI and voice lifecycle converged on one shared VRAM core and systemd-unit-managed voice services to avoid vLLM orphan leaks.
questions: Was the litellm-proxy.service broken state fixed after being flagged?
Was the reciprocal joint sanity pass (voice load + model company up) ever run?
What is the exact commit hash or state of the merged interactive-surface branch?
Was the AssemblyAI key actually moved into ~/.company/.secrets?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a note from ops to voice to coordinate shared-ground changes; actual content is a detailed decision-and-verification log that records convergence. The two match.
apparent_themes: single source of truth for VRAM arbitration
systemd cgroup as the orphan-safe teardown mechanism
intention_signals: The file is trying to align two independently built resource managers into one shared core and to make voice services unit-managed so that vLLM EngineCore processes are always reaped cleanly, preventing VRAM orphan leaks.
novelty: It is an unusual runtime coordination artifact: a Markdown 'letter' between sessions that doubles as a canonical decision record and live verification log for system architecture changes.
contribution: It preserves the negotiated design (shared cli/gpu.py core, unit-managed voice, cgroup teardown recipe, .secrets format) and the verification evidence that the convergence works, which a fused system would keep as the authoritative rationale.
summary_for_embedding: A cross-session coordination note recording the convergence of the company CLI's VRAM manager and the voice lifecycle onto a shared cli/gpu.py core, with voice services switched to systemd units to avoid vLLM EngineCore orphan leaks.
intention_for_embedding: To align the ops and voice sessions on a single orphan-safe VRAM resource manager and to document the resulting registry, unit, and lifecycle changes.
